summary: Cleaned up Sheik fighter motion-state definitions by replacing raw hexadecimal motion flag and move-id constants in `ftSk_Init_MotionStateTable` with named enum values and shifted `FtMoveId_*` expressions. The PR also introduced a Sheik-specific `ftSk_MotionFlags` enum in `forward.h` composed from existing `FtStateChange_*` bits, added the needed header include, and converted a few hexadecimal item/state numeric literals to decimal. No review discussion or PR body text was present in the slice.
what_changed: Added `#include "ft/forward.h"` to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/forward.h` so the new Sheik motion flag enum can reference `FtStateChange_*` definitions.

Added `typedef enum ftSk_MotionFlags` in `forward.h`, defining reusable named combinations such as `ftSk_MF_Special`, `ftSk_MF_SpecialN`, `ftSk_MF_SpecialAirN`, `ftSk_MF_SpecialSLoop`, and `ftSk_MF_SpecialAirHi`.
Updated `ftSk_Init_MotionStateTable` in `ftSk_Init.c` to use `ftSk_MF_*` names instead of raw flag hex values like `0x00340111`, `0x003C0111`, `0x00340412`, and `0x00340613`.

Updated motion-state move-id fields from raw shifted hex constants such as `0x12000000`, `0x13000000`, `0x14000000`, and `0x15000000` to explicit expressions like `FtMoveId_SpecialN << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialS << 24`, `FtMoveId_SpecialHi << 24`, and `FtMoveId_SpecialLw << 24`.
Added `#include "forward.h"` to `ftSk_Init.c` so the motion-state table can use the new `ftSk_MF_*` symbols.

Converted several numeric literals in `ftSk_Init_OnLoad` and `ftSk_Init_UnkMotionStates4` from hexadecimal form to decimal: `0x4F`, `0x50`, `0x55`, `0x61`, and `0x56` became `79`, `80`, `85`, `97`, and `86`.
smart_moves: Factored repeated bitmask combinations into named Sheik-specific motion flag constants, making the table easier to audit for ground/air, loop/non-loop, and special-move variants.
Built the new flags compositionally from existing `FtStateChange_*` names instead of assigning opaque numeric values, preserving the exact values while documenting intent.
Kept the motion-state table structurally unchanged and replaced only constant expressions, which is a low-risk cleanup pattern for decomp code because it should not alter generated data values.
Used `FtMoveId_* << 24` to expose the semantic encoding of the move-id field rather than leaving pre-shifted magic numbers in the table.
Centralized Sheik motion flag names in `forward.h`, making them reusable by other Sheik source files without duplicating bitmask expressions.
decomp_lessons: For fighter motion tables, replace raw flag words with named enum constants only after decomposing them into already-known `FtStateChange_*` bits; this improves readability without changing table layout.
Shifted move-id fields are clearer as `FtMoveId_* << 24` than as raw high-byte hex constants, and this convention makes mismatched special-move classifications easier to spot.
Character-specific `*_MotionFlags` enums can encode common flag families such as base special, aerial special, and loop states while still relying on global engine flag names.

When a cleanup is intended to be behavior-preserving, keep table ordering, callback names, animation IDs, and other data fields untouched; this PR only renamed/re-expressed constants in the motion-state definitions.
Header placement matters: shared character enums used by init tables belong in a forward/header file with the correct includes for the enum dependencies.
naming_conventions: Character-specific motion flag enum type uses the pattern `ftSk_MotionFlags`.
Sheik motion flag constants use the prefix `ftSk_MF_` followed by the move/state family, for example `ftSk_MF_SpecialN`, `ftSk_MF_SpecialAirS`, and `ftSk_MF_SpecialNLoop`.
Motion-state enum names in the existing code use `ftSk_MS_` and descriptive special-move suffixes such as `SpecialN_ChargeStart` and `SpecialN_ChargeLoop`.
Move-id constants use the global `FtMoveId_Special*` naming scheme and are shifted into the motion-state table field with `<< 24`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use named enum expressions for constants where the compiler will emit the same integer values, enabling source cleanup without affecting matching output.
Represent bitmask constants as OR-combinations of existing flags, e.g. `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateModel | FtStateChange_SkipUpdateItemVis | FtStateChange_Unk_UpdatePhys | FtStateChange_FreezeState`.
Preserve exact motion-state table values by deriving air variants with `FtStateChange_SkipUpdateParasol`, loop variants with `FtStateChange_Unk_19`, and move-id fields with `FtMoveId_* << 24`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice, so no reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter `*_Init_MotionStateTable` definitions still using raw hex flag constants instead of named `FtStateChange_*` combinations.
Search for other character-specific motion flag enums to align naming and factoring patterns with `ftSk_MotionFlags`.
Audit shifted move-id constants in motion tables and replace raw values with `FtMoveId_* << 24` where behavior-preserving.
